threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand on the left side of the escalator, unaware that's a rookie mistake. Locals glide right by you like it’s a game.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. Even the driver was nodding along. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an old lady trading homemade jam for fresh herbs at the backyard market—I knew Neukölln had stolen my heart.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a street mural that doubles as a tiny food market on Sundays, with local artists selling handmade goods right on the sidewalk.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know the back alley short cuts to avoid the 24th Street crowds on taco Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy feeding his pet parrot a pretzel on the 5 bus today. Parrot was perfectly calm, just enjoying the ride. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about trendy bars, but it’s the tiny little bakeries in hidden corners that serve the best ensaïmades.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their pronunciation of 'Sagrada Família'—it’s always a dead giveaway when they stress the 'a' too much.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it ‘the Tube’ instead of ‘the Overground’—everyone knows the Overground is the real way to get around here.
Caught a late 50 last night; the driver was belting out 90s R&B. Half the bus was vibing, while the other half just stared, baffled. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One night, I heard a saxophonist jamming on the street while a group of kids played soccer under the flickering streetlight—classic Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how people in Friedrichshain will host BBQs in the park even in winter? Just braving the cold like they’re invincible. Classic Berlin spirit!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they instinctively know to breathe through their mouth when walking past a taco truck line. Truly an art form.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a fedora playing sax on the bus today—he got off at 6th & Market. Muni's the only place where that makes perfect sense.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks and plazas, but the real vibe is in those tiny artisanal shops hidden on side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look baffled when they see two people sharing a single umbrella on a rainy day. Classic sign of a newbie here.
